report_name: Redwood AI Enters Post-Quantum Cryptography Market with Acquisition of Quantum.IQ

vulnerabilities.name: Weakness of Public-Key Cryptography to Quantum Attacks
vulnerabilities.description: Public-key encryption algorithms such as RSA and ECC are vulnerable to being broken by sufficiently powerful quantum computers. This threat enables 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks, where adversaries capture encrypted data today for future decryption.

identities.name: Redwood AI Corp.
identities.description: An artificial intelligence company that announced its planned acquisition of Quantum.IQ Technologies to expand into the post-quantum cryptography market.

identities.name: Quantum.IQ Technologies Inc.
identities.description: A Vancouver-based company specializing in AI-driven, post-quantum cryptography (PQC) solutions, which was acquired by Redwood AI Corp.

identities.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
identities.description: A U.S. government body mentioned as standardizing post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms.

relationships.target_name: Weakness of Public-Key Cryptography to Quantum Attacks

relationships.source_name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
